brand_rankings::f8446e14ae91688168499ce1: Oatly ranks first with a composite score of 61.79, driven by the highest normalized trends average (100.0) and strong YouTube engagement (80.28), while Califia Farms follows at 59.83 with the top sentiment score among the group (66.815) and leading wikimedia momentum. Oatly's search and video strength signals broad consumer awareness, but Califia Farms' sentiment and momentum advantage suggests stronger brand affinity worth amplifying through content strategy.
sentiment_heatmap::e2b2fe3a6e4b24704ec36: LIVE Soda Kombucha leads all brands in text volume at 194 mentions and posts a strong VADER compound score of 0.31, while GT's Living Foods carries the highest negative sentiment share at 11.3%. Health-Ade's low negative rate of 4% paired with a compound score of 0.33 signals a loyal, satisfied audience worth targeting for retention and advocacy campaigns.
trends_chart::dee700d969b6250e064d4abe84: Califia Farms dominates CA search interest with an average score of 22.9, while GT's Living Foods trails significantly at 1.1 and the remaining three brands register near-zero activity. Califia Farms holds near-exclusive organic search visibility in this set, making it the benchmark competitor any challenger brand must close the gap against to gain digital discoverability.
purchase_channels::49b6354df8b42c65c3281: LIVE Soda Kombucha shows the highest combined online-plus-both signal at 70.0% (46.7% online, 23.3% both), while Humm Kombucha has the lowest online-only share at 30.0% with zero in-store-only signals. The category skews heavily digital across all five brands, making e-commerce and omnichannel retail placement the primary battleground for purchase conversion.
youtube_engagement::bb88f253da0bed82115a: LIVE Soda Kombucha leads YouTube engagement with 40.2M total views and 12,350 comments across 16 videos, matching GT's Living Foods' perfect engagement score of 100.0 despite GT's drawing 31.2M views from 18 videos. Health-Ade and Humm Kombucha show critically low view and comment volumes, signaling underinvestment in video content that limits their organic discovery among CA's digitally active health consumers.
wikimedia_momentum::0acf66e3092baed231f6: GT's Living Foods shows the strongest Wikipedia momentum at +80.46% with an average of 25.9 monthly views, while Brew Dr. Kombucha averages 331.1 views but is declining at -10.78%. GT's accelerating interest signals rising consumer curiosity worth supporting with educational content, whereas Brew Dr. should investigate why higher-volume traffic is eroding.
